text.title: Rust Error Handling Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure: classify crate → pick library → write Error type → swap unwraps → wire clippy gate.

step.name: classify-crate
step.action: Read `[lib]` / `[[bin]]` / `build = "build.rs"`. If both `[lib]` and `[[bin]]` present, classify by which is published.

step.name: pick-library
step.action: library → `thiserror`. binary → `anyhow`. build-script → `Box<dyn Error>`. Add the chosen crate to `[dependencies]`.

step.name: write-error-type
step.input: error_library + list of fallible call sites from `cargo check`
step.action: For thiserror: copy `templates/error.rs.thiserror.tmpl`, add variants per call site, wire `#[from]` for upstream errors. For anyhow: define `pub type Result<T> = anyhow::Result<T>` and call `.context("…")` at each `?`.

step.name: swap-unwraps
step.action: Replace each call with `?` after `.context(...)` (anyhow) or chained `Err.into()` (thiserror). Run `cargo test` after each module.
step.decision-gate: If a call site genuinely cannot fail (e.g. `regex::Regex::new` on a compile-time-constant pattern): use `expect("static regex compiles")` and add a unit test that exercises the path. Document with comment `// SAFETY: compile-time constant`.

step.name: wire-clippy-gate
step.action: Add `templates/clippy.toml` to crate root. Add CI step: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D clippy::unwrap_used -D clippy::expect_used`. Tests escape the gate via `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` if needed.
step.output: locked gate; new unwraps in `src/` fail PR
